untranslatable_reason: This is a portfolio optimization framework (Modern Portfolio Theory / Markowitz), not a position sizing rule. It describes how to allocate weights across N securities to achieve a target return while minimizing variance using Lagrangian constrained optimization. It does not specify HOW MUCH capital to risk per trade, how to scale positions based on performance/volatility/drawdown, or any of the standard sizing methods (Kelly, Optimal f, Fixed Fractional, etc.). The rule is about asset allocation weighting, not position sizing in the trading sense.
